F,q,rn Porrrrcal Pucrrcns CouurssroN
P.O. Box 807 . 428 J Street . Sacramento. CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 . Fax (916) 322-0886
March 29,2001
Antonio Camcam, Chairman
Conception Life Committee
1109 San Rafael Avenue, #14
Glendale, CA9l202
Re:
Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-01-062
Dear Mr. Camcam:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the provisions of the
Political Refprm Act (the "Act").r You asked what informational material on the
Political Reform Act is available for committees.
A committee has several sources of information available through the Fair
Political Practices Commission. The Commission's website, www.fppc.ca.gov, contains
up-to-date information on pending regulatory matters concerning all aspects of the Act.
We are currently embarking on a major review of campaign disclosure rules. I have
added your name and address to our Interested Persons List, which will notifu you of
upcoming events, as well as provide information on the project as it progresses.
Additionally, you may contact us in one of the following ways
1. The FPPC website also contains the agendas for past and future Commission
meetings and instructions on how to obtain relevant materials. Question and
Answer sheets designed to address common questions to the Commission are
available as well.
2. For more particular advice, you may reach a Commission consultant and
receive telephone advice by calling 866-ASK-FPPC. While this advice does
I Govemment Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at Tifle 2, sections
18109-18996, of the California Code of Regulations.
6Ll


File No. 01-062
Page No. 2
not afford the immunity provided recipients of written advice, telephone
advice is ideal for routine questions that can be answered in a short time.
3. You may either provide or request information by submitting a facsimile
addressed to the Campaign Reporting Task Force at (916) 327-2026
or via U.S. Mail to the Campaign Reporting Task Force at P.O. Box 807,
Sacramento, CA. 95812-0807.
I have enclosed a copy of Regulation 18329 which will assist you should you
need to submit a request for written advice.
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
By:
Joan Giannetta
Secretary, Legal
tie
Enclosure

sections.question: You asked what informational material on the Political Reform Act is available for committees.
sections.question_synthetic: What informational resources and materials related to the Political Reform Act are available to political committees?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC provides multiple resources including its website (www.fppc.ca.gov), an Interested Persons List for notifications, telephone advice via 866-ASK-FPPC, and written advice through the Campaign Reporting Task Force via facsimile or mail.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Informal advice letter without formal QUESTION and CONCLUSION section headers. Question and conclusion extracted from body text. Document provides directory of resources rather than legal analysis of a specific compliance question.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: You asked what informational material on the Political Reform Act is available for committees.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC provides multiple resources including its website (www.fppc.ca.gov), an Interested Persons List for notifications, telephone advice via 866-ASK-FPPC, and written advice through the Campaign Reporting Task Force via facsimile or mail.

embedding.summary: The FPPC responds to a committee chairman's inquiry about available informational materials on the Political Reform Act by directing him to multiple resources including the FPPC website, telephone consultation, and written advice procedures.
